They provide a packet_id and then status values: route_verdict=CONTINUE, repair_target=resolve prior blocker ids before ALLOW: ART-001-BLK-45E2B386, blocked_move=do not silently drop source-grounded blockers. They ask to return a plain text record with three key=value lines: route_verdict, repair_target, blocked_move. No extra headings, etc. So we output exactly:

repair_target=resolve prior blocker ids before ALLOW: ART-001-BLK-45E2B386
blocked_move=do not silently drop source-grounded blockers

But must be three lines exactly. Let's verify: Should we include the packet_id? The user said "Return the three-line status record." Fields are route_verdict, repair_target, blocked_move. So only those three lines. No packet_id. Should we preserve exactly the values? route_verdict=CONTINUE, repair_target=resolve prior blocker ids before ALLOW: ART-001-BLK-45E2B386, blocked_move=do not silently drop source-grounded blockers. Yes.

Thus answer is:

route_verdict=CONTINUE
repair_target=resolve prior blocker ids before ALLOW: ART-001-BLK-45E2B386
blocked_move=do not silently drop source-grounded blockers

We should not add any extra content. Ensure no extra spaces. Also we must not include the "packet_id". Straight three lines. That is it.
</think>
